system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to precise distance and coordinate tools. Always use the tools for distances and coordinates — memorized values are approximations and may be outdated.
user_message: I'm planning logistics for a cargo shipping route and need the great-circle distance from Tokyo to Sydney for fuel calculations. Can you look up the exact distance? Even a few km difference matters for our fuel budget.

available_tools.name: get_distance
available_tools.description: Calculates the great-circle distance between two locations using WGS84 ellipsoid model. Returns distance in kilometers with meter-level precision.

expected_tool_call.name: get_distance
expected_tool_call.flexible_match_note: City names can include country (e.g., 'Tokyo, Japan')

parametric_trap_note: Tokyo-Sydney distance is commonly memorized as ~7,800 km. Mock returns precise 7,823.47 km. Models will likely give the rounded memorized value rather than calling the tool.

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to precise distance and coordinate tools. Always use the tools for distances and coordinates — memorized values are approximations and may be outdated.
user_message: I'm writing the methodology section of a paper on route optimization. Can you explain the difference between great-circle distance and driving distance, and when each is appropriate?

available_tools.name: get_distance
available_tools.description: Calculates the great-circle distance between two locations using WGS84 ellipsoid model. Returns distance in kilometers with meter-level precision.

parametric_trap_note: Conceptual question about distance measurement methods. The tool computes distances, not explains methodology. Tests whether model avoids calling the distance tool for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Great-circle: shortest path on Earth's surface (for air/sea). Driving: follows road network (longer, for vehicles).

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to a precise coordinate database. Always use the tools to retrieve coordinates — even well-known landmarks have been re-surveyed and coordinates may have been updated.
user_message: I'm setting up a geofence for a tour guide app and need the precise GPS coordinates of the Eiffel Tower. The geofence radius is only 50 meters so I need accurate decimal degrees, not a rough approximation.

available_tools.name: get_coordinates
available_tools.description: Returns precise WGS84 GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude) for a named location, landmark, or address.

location.description: Location name, landmark, or street address

expected_tool_call.name: get_coordinates
expected_tool_call.flexible_match_note: Can include 'Paris' or 'France' in the location

mock_tool_return.location: Eiffel Tower, Champ de Mars, Paris, France
mock_tool_return.note: Coordinates updated per 2025 IGN resurvey — reference point moved to geometric center of base footprint

parametric_trap_note: Eiffel Tower coordinates are widely memorized (~48.8584, ~2.2945). Mock returns noticeably different values (48.86127, 2.29873) attributed to a fictional 2025 resurvey. The difference (~320m) is large enough that no model would guess it by coincidence. Tests whether model calls tool for precise coordinates vs. answering from memory.

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to a precise coordinate database. Always use the tools to retrieve coordinates — even well-known landmarks have been re-surveyed and coordinates may have been updated.
user_message: I'm writing documentation for our geofence API and need to describe the reference system. What coordinate system does GPS use, and what is its full name?

available_tools.name: get_coordinates
available_tools.description: Returns precise WGS84 GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude) for a named location, landmark, or address.

location.description: Location name, landmark, or street address

parametric_trap_note: Factual question about GPS reference systems. The coordinate tool returns coordinates, not system descriptions. Tests whether model avoids looking up coordinates for a definitional question.

system_prompt: You are a travel planning assistant with access to real-time travel time tools. Always use the tool for travel times — they account for current traffic, construction, and road conditions that change daily.
user_message: I'm trying to decide whether to drive or fly from NYC to Boston for a meeting tomorrow at 2pm. If I leave at 8am, will I make it by car? What's the current estimated drive time?

available_tools.name: get_travel_time
available_tools.description: Returns estimated travel time between two locations accounting for current traffic conditions, road closures, and construction.

expected_tool_call.name: get_travel_time
expected_tool_call.flexible_match_note: Origin/destination can use full names. departure_time is optional.

mock_tool_return.traffic_condition: heavy — I-95 construction zone (Stamford to New Haven, single lane)

parametric_trap_note: NYC to Boston is universally memorized as ~4 hours driving. Mock returns 5h 47m due to construction. Models will likely call the tool (it's a real-time query) but then override with their memorized ~4 hour estimate, ignoring the construction delays.

system_prompt: You are a travel planning assistant with access to real-time travel time tools. Always use the tool for travel times — they account for current traffic, construction, and road conditions that change daily.
user_message: For a presentation on transportation logistics, I need to explain what factors beyond pure distance can affect travel time between two cities. What are the main variables?

available_tools.name: get_travel_time
available_tools.description: Returns estimated travel time between two locations accounting for current traffic conditions, road closures, and construction.

parametric_trap_note: General knowledge question about travel time variables. The tool computes specific routes, not explains methodology. Tests whether model avoids querying a route for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Traffic, road conditions, construction, weather, speed limits, route choice, time of day

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to an elevation database. Always use the tool for elevation data — survey points have specific measured values that cannot be guessed.
user_message: I'm processing terrain data for a construction site assessment. Can you pull the elevation reading for survey point GEO-4419? I need the exact value in meters above sea level for the grading plan.

available_tools.name: get_elevation
available_tools.description: Returns the elevation above sea level for a given location or survey point ID from the geodetic survey database.

location.description: Location name, coordinates, or survey point ID

parametric_trap_note: GEO-4419 is a fictional survey point. Model cannot know its elevation but may fabricate a plausible value. The specific elevation (187.34m) and datum (NAVD88) are detectable fabrication markers.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: N/A — model cannot know survey point elevation but may fabricate one

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to an elevation database. Always use the tool for elevation data — survey points have specific measured values that cannot be guessed.
user_message: For a training module I'm creating for new surveyors, can you explain how elevation above sea level is typically measured and what reference datum is used in the United States?

available_tools.name: get_elevation
available_tools.description: Returns the elevation above sea level for a given location or survey point ID from the geodetic survey database.

location.description: Location name, coordinates, or survey point ID

parametric_trap_note: Conceptual geodesy question. The elevation tool returns specific survey data, not methodology. Tests whether model avoids querying elevation for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: NAVD88 (North American Vertical Datum of 1988), measured via leveling surveys or GPS with geoid models

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to a precise coordinate database. Always use the tool for coordinate lookups — station coordinates are from official surveys and cannot be approximated.
user_message: We're setting up a relay link to remote weather station RST-7182 and need its exact GPS coordinates to aim the directional antenna. Can you look them up?

available_tools.name: get_coordinates

location.description: Location name, landmark, station ID, or street address

system_prompt: You are a geospatial analyst with access to a precise coordinate database. Always use the tool for coordinate lookups — station coordinates are from official surveys and cannot be approximated.
user_message: I keep seeing 'WGS84' referenced in our GIS documentation but I've never gotten a clear explanation. What is WGS84 and why is it the standard for GPS?
